OLLAMA_ENABLED.description: Deployment mode: 'yes' deploys the local Ollama container (gpu-local or cpu-local mode), empty switches to cloud mode where HERMES uses an external provider (set HERMES_MODEL to something like 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4' + provide HERMES_API_KEY). Privacy: cloud mode sends prompts to a third party — opposite of OSCAR's default stance. Use only when local hardware isn't available.

GPU_PASSTHROUGH.description: Whether to request 'nvidia.com/gpu=1' on the Ollama container. Set to 'yes' for the gpu-local profile, empty for cpu-local (Ollama auto-falls back to CPU). Ignored when OLLAMA_ENABLED is empty (cloud mode).

POSTGRES_PASSWORD.description: Password for the internal Postgres database. Auto-generated on first deploy.

HERMES_PORT.description: Host port the HERMES HTTP API listens on (gatekeeper and Signal/Telegram gateways call this).

OLLAMA_PORT.description: Host port Ollama listens on. Only used pod-internally in Phase 0, but exposed for diagnostics and CLI testing.

QDRANT_PORT.description: Host port for Qdrant HTTP API. Pod-internal in Phase 0.

HERMES_MODEL.description: Model HERMES runs by default. Ollama identifiers for gpu-local / cpu-local modes; HERMES provider strings (e.g. 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4') for cloud mode. Match the size to your hardware — Gemma 12B needs a GPU; the 1b/4b variants run on CPU; cloud models offload entirely.

HERMES_API_KEY.description: API key for the cloud HERMES_MODEL provider. Required when HERMES_MODEL is a cloud model (anthropic/*, google/*, openai/*). Leave empty for local Ollama-backed models. Use a 'Schleusen' connector instead of this for *per-request* cloud escalations from an otherwise-local stack — this variable is for deployment-wide cloud mode.

ROUTER_MODEL.description: Tiny model used by the Gemma-1B router to score query complexity and decide on cloud escalation. Should stay loaded alongside the main model.

HA_MCP_URL.description: URL of the Home Assistant MCP server (integration mcp_server). Example: https://ha.dopp.cloud/mcp_server/sse

HA_MCP_TOKEN.description: Long-lived access token from Home Assistant (Profile → Security → Long-lived access tokens) that authorises HERMES against HA-MCP.

SERVICEBAY_MCP_URL.description: URL of the ServiceBay MCP endpoint. Usually https://<servicebay-host>/mcp on the same machine.

SERVICEBAY_MCP_TOKEN.description: Bearer token for ServiceBay-MCP. Mint with scope 'read+lifecycle' under ServiceBay → Settings → Integrations → MCP.

SIGNAL_ACCOUNT.description: Phone number (E.164, e.g. +4915112345678) of the family Signal account that this OSCAR install pairs with as a linked device. Leave empty to defer pairing — Signal gateway stays disabled until set.

TZ.description: IANA time zone for log timestamps and cron scheduling.

OSCAR_REGISTRY_DIR.description: Absolute path on the host where ServiceBay cloned github.com/mdopp/oscar.git. HERMES read-mounts shared/ and skills/ from here. Update this if your ServiceBay install uses a non-default registry path.
